agent_output: {"integration_assessment":{"component_interactions":[{"from":"HTTP ingestion endpoint","to":"internal event pipeline","status":"correct","issue":"Well-instrumented with request IDs and tracing"},{"from":"event pipeline","to":"data warehouse","status":"broken","issue":"Writes are not idempotent. Combined with at-least-once delivery and retry logic (3 retries with backoff), duplicate records are guaranteed under normal operation — not just edge cases. Every ACK timeout or circuit breaker recovery produces phantom rows."},{"from":"event pipeline","to":"dead-letter queue","status":"broken","issue":"DLQ exists but has no consumer. Failed events silently accumulate with no reprocessing, no alerting on queue depth, and no retention policy. This is a silent data loss path disguised as resilience."},{"from":"retry mechanism","to":"data warehouse","status":"risk","issue":"Exponential backoff retries amplify the duplication problem. Each retry that succeeds after a lost ACK creates a duplicate. The circuit breaker recovery burst can also replay events already written."}],"contract_compliance":"HTTP ingestion contract appears sound. The contract between pipeline and warehouse is violated — the system promises event delivery but actually delivers event-plus-duplicates with no way for downstream consumers to distinguish.","data_flow_integrity":"Compromised. The combination of at-least-once delivery, non-idempotent writes, and no deduplication means the data warehouse will contain an unknowable number of duplicate records. Any analytics built on this data are unreliable. This is not a theoretical concern — it is the expected steady-state behavior."},"observability_review":{"logging":{"coverage":"adequate","gaps":[]},"metrics":{"coverage":"Strong — Prometheus histograms for latency, p99 dashboards and alerts configured","gaps":["No metric tracking duplicate event writes","No metric on DLQ depth or age of oldest message","No metric distinguishing first-attempt writes from retry writes"]},"tracing":{"coverage":"Good — OpenTelemetry distributed tracing with request ID propagation","gaps":["Traces should tag retry attempts so duplicates are identifiable in post-hoc analysis"]},"debugging_capability":"Good for request-level debugging. Poor for detecting and quantifying the duplication problem — an on-call engineer would not know duplicates are accumulating without dedicated metrics."},"resilience_assessment":{"failure_modes":[{"scenario":"ACK lost after successful warehouse write, event retried","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"MUST-HAVE: Implement idempotent writes using a unique event ID as a natural deduplication key. Use INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING or equivalent upsert pattern in the warehouse."},{"scenario":"Dead-letter queue fills up indefinitely","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"MUST-HAVE: Deploy a DLQ consumer that either retries with backoff or routes to a human review path. Add alerting on queue depth > threshold and oldest message age."},{"scenario":"Circuit breaker opens during warehouse outage, events buffered then replayed","handling":"partial","recommendation":"The replay after circuit breaker close will duplicate any events that were partially written. Idempotency key resolves this."},{"scenario":"Sustained load above 10k events/sec","handling":"partial","recommendation":"Load tested at 10k/sec for 30 min. Consider longer soak tests (hours) and burst tests above capacity to validate backpressure behavior."}],"retry_config":"Exponential backoff with 3 retries — mechanically sound but actively harmful without idempotent writes. Every successful retry after a lost ACK creates a duplicate.","timeout_config":"Not specified but assumed present given the overall instrumentation quality.","circuit_breakers":"Present on warehouse writes — good. Recovery path needs idempotency to avoid replay duplication.","graceful_degradation":"Partial. DLQ provides a safety net for failed events but the net has a hole — nothing ever reads from it."},"operational_readiness":{"deployment_ready":false,"rollback_capable":true,"rollback_plan":"Blue/green deployment with load balancer traffic switch enables instant rollback. This is well-designed.","monitoring_coverage":"Strong for availability and latency. Blind to data correctness — no duplicate detection, no DLQ depth alerting.","on_call_documentation":"Needs runbook entries for: (1) how to identify and remediate duplicate records, (2) DLQ processing procedures, (3) circuit breaker recovery verification.","new_infrastructure_required":{"new_env_vars":[],"new_services":["DLQ consumer/processor service"],"new_queues_topics":[],"config_changes":["Add unique event ID column and unique constraint to warehouse table for idempotent upserts"],"infra_provisioning":["DLQ alerting rule on queue depth and message age"]},"database_safety":{"has_migrations":true,"reversible":true,"data_preserving":true,"large_table_impact":true,"details":"Adding a unique constraint for idempotency on a high-volume clickstream table requires careful migration — may need to deduplicate existing rows first, and index creation on a large table can lock writes. Use CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY or equivalent."},"verdict":"NOT READY"},"escalations":[{"type":"integration_gap","issue":"Non-idempotent writes with at-least-once delivery guarantees data corruption","reason":"This is not an edge case — it is the expected behavior of the system. Every lost ACK, every circuit breaker recovery, every network hiccup produces duplicate records in the warehouse. Analytics built on this data will be wrong. Idempotent writes via a unique event ID and upsert pattern are required before shipping.","routed_to":"MJ"},{"type":"integration_gap","issue":"Dead-letter queue has no consumer — silent data loss path","reason":"The DLQ was designed as a safety net but nothing reads from it. Failed events accumulate silently. Without a consumer and alerting, this is functionally equivalent to dropping events on the floor but with extra steps. A DLQ consumer and depth-based alerting are required.","routed_to":"MJ"}],"confidence":{"level":60,"high_confidence_areas":["Observability instrumentation is production-grade","Deployment and rollback strategy is sound","The duplication problem is certain, not speculative — it follows directly from at-least-once + non-idempotent writes"],"low_confidence_areas":["Timeout configuration not explicitly described","Backpressure behavior above tested capacity","Warehouse schema details and migration complexity for adding idempotency"],"assumptions":["Events have or can be assigned a unique ID suitable for deduplication","The data warehouse supports upsert or ON CONFLICT semantics","The 10k events/sec load test reflects realistic production traffic patterns"]}}

content.thinking:
Let me assess this real-time analytics ingestion service based on the signals provided.

Classification: `none` — sufficient context exists to make a determination.

Key issues:
1. Non-idempotent writes + at-least-once delivery = guaranteed data corruption over time
2. Dead-letter queue with no consumer = silent data loss
3. These are fundamental correctness issues, not nice-to-haves
